name: Sierra de Villanueva de Sigena - Refuge of Piedrafita from Monasterio de Sigena
name_original: Sierra de Villanueva de Sigena - Refugio de Piedrafita desde Monasterio de Sigena
region_intro: Hiking around Villanueva De Sigena is characterized by its unique semi-desert landscapes within the Monegros region of Huesca, Aragon. The terrain features arid, ochre-colored expanses, sculpted by wind and sun, offering a distinctive natural environment. Notable features include the Sierra de Sena, which reaches 595 meters, and the Tozales de Monegros, remarkable multi-colored rock formations shaped by erosion. The area also includes the Alcanadre River and scattered pine forests, providing varied natural elements for exploration. Best hiking trails around Villanueva De Sigena The most popular hiking route is Tozal Solitario from Albalatillo — circular through the Monegros, a 15.5 miles (25.0 km) trail that takes 6 hours 34 minutes to complete. This difficult route traverses the unique Monegros semi-desert landscape. Another top favourite among local hikers is Sierra de Villanueva de Sigena - Refuge of Piedrafita from Monasterio de Sigena, a difficult 13.8 miles (22.3 km) path. This trail leads through the Sierra de Villanueva de Sigena and past the Refuge of Piedrafita. Local hikers also love the Tozal de la Cobeta loop from Ermita de San Miguel, a 3.9 miles (6.3 km) trail leading through the area around Tozal de la Cobeta, often completed in about 1 hour 43 minutes. Hiking around Villanueva De Sigena is defined by arid Monegros terrain, eroded rock formations, and elevated viewpoints. The network offers options for easy and difficult routes. The routes in Villanueva De Sigena are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 10 reviews. More than 30 hikers have used komoot to explore Villanueva De Sigena's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on hikes around Villanueva de Sigena?
A: Hiking around Villanueva de Sigena offers a distinctive experience through its semi-desert landscapes within the Monegros region. You'll encounter arid, ochre-colored terrain, sculpted by wind and sun, with remarkable eroded rock formations known as &lt;strong&gt;Tozales de Monegros&lt;/strong&gt;. The area also features the Sierra de Sena mountain range and some scattered pine forests, providing a varied natural environment.

Q: Are there any easy hiking trails suitable for beginners or casual walkers?
A: Yes, there are easy options available. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e947118675/laguna-de-sarinena-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna de Sariñena Loop&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 7.6 km trail. Another accessible option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24550472" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tozal de la Cobeta loop from Ermita de San Miguel&lt;/a&gt;, which is 6.3 km long and typically takes less than two hours to complete.

Q: What are some notable natural features or landmarks to look out for on the trails?
A: The region is rich in unique natural features. You can explore the striking multi-colored rock formations of the &lt;strong&gt;Tozales de Monegros&lt;/strong&gt;, including the prominent &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5771760" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tozal de la Cobeta&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2105057" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Pilón (Sierra de Sena Summit)&lt;/a&gt; is a significant point on many trails, offering panoramic views. You might also encounter the historic Romanesque bridge over the Alcanadre River.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes around Villanueva de Sigena?
A: Yes, several circular routes are available. A popular choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1091909324/tozal-solitario-from-albalatillo-circular-through-the-monegros" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tozal Solitario from Albalatillo — circular through the Monegros&lt;/a&gt;, a challenging 25 km trail. For a shorter option, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24550472" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tozal de la Cobeta loop from Ermita de San Miguel&lt;/a&gt;, which is 6.3 km.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Villanueva de Sigena?
A: The trails around Villanueva de Sigena are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 4.6 stars from over 10 reviews. Hikers often praise the unique semi-desert landscapes, the solitude of the Monegros region, and the rewarding views from elevated points like the Sierra de Sena.

Q: Are there any viewpoints offering scenic vistas of the area?
A: Yes, the area boasts several viewpoints that provide breathtaking vistas of the diverse landscapes. The &lt;strong&gt;Mirador del Pilón&lt;/strong&gt; on the Sierra de Sena offers expansive panoramic views across the entire province of Huesca. Other notable viewpoints include Mirador de la Mora, Mirador de la Sierra, and Mirador del Castillo, which are excellent for appreciating the unique Monegros terrain.

Q: Is there a good time of year to go hiking in Villanueva de Sigena?
A: The Monegros region is characterized by arid conditions, constant wind, and sun. Spring and autumn generally offer the most comfortable temperatures for hiking. Summers can be very hot, making early morning or late afternoon hikes preferable, while winters can be chilly but offer a different perspective on the landscape.

Q: Are there any shelters or refuges along the hiking trails?
A: Yes, hikers can find the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2105056" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Refugio Piedrafita&lt;/a&gt;, a typical stone shelter in the Monegros region. It offers a welcoming spot with tables, benches, a fireplace, and a bathroom. Access to its interior can be arranged through the Villanueva de Sigena Town Council.

Q: Can I find trails that lead to historical or cultural sites?
A: While the focus is on natural landscapes, some routes connect to historical points. For example, a cycling route connects Villanueva de Sigena with the historic Royal Monastery of Santa María de Sigena, allowing you to immerse yourself in the Monegrino landscape while visiting a significant cultural site.

Q: What are the longest and most challenging hikes in the area?
A: One of the longest and most challenging hikes is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1091909324/tozal-solitario-from-albalatillo-circular-through-the-monegros" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tozal Solitario from Albalatillo — circular through the Monegros&lt;/a&gt;, a difficult 25 km trail that takes over 6 hours to complete. Another demanding route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e947176164/sierra-de-villanueva-de-sigena-refuge-of-piedrafita-from-monasterio-de-sigena" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra de Villanueva de Sigena - Refuge of Piedrafita from Monasterio de Sigena&lt;/a&gt;, a difficult 22.3 km path through the Sierra de Villanueva de Sigena.

Q: Are there any specific regulations or permits needed for hiking in the Villanueva de Sigena area?
A: Generally, no specific permits are required for hiking on the established trails in the Villanueva de Sigena area. However, it's always advisable to respect local signage, stay on marked paths, and be mindful of the semi-desert environment, especially regarding fire prevention and waste disposal.
tips: It is a small stone shelter typical of the Monegros region, designed to provide shelter for shepherds or hikers in bad weather.

Its construction, from the 12th century, was ordered by Sancha de Castilla for the hospital sisters. Therefore, even though it was a double monastery (with a part for nuns and another for friars), the highest authority was always in the hands of the nuns, who at various times were women linked to royalty or the Aragonese nobility.
